The summer festival (Hirano Danjiri Festival) is held annually from July 11 to 14, restricting traffic on nearby {{ill|National Route 25|ja|国道25号|qq=draft}}. == History == {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観|qq=draft}} 4 ({{ill|862|ja|862nd year|qq=draft}}), {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂|qq=draft}}'s grandson {{ill|Sakanoue no Tōdō|ja|坂上当道|qq=draft}} established the shrine to {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō|qq=draft}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) which became the First Main Hall. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft}}}} Main deities worshipped: * First Main Hall - {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō|qq=draft}}) * Second Main Hall - {{ill|Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉册尊|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja|速玉男尊|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kotosaka-no-Mikoto|ja|事解男尊|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Kumano Sansho Gongen|ja|熊野三所権現|qq=draft}}) * Third Main Hall - {{ill|Izanagi-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉諾尊|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Kumano Shōjō Gongen|ja|熊野證誠権現|qq=draft}}) == External Links == * [https://kumata.jp/ Kumata Shrine] [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|杭全神社|version=104231843|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka City]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Kasuga-zukuri architecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Osaka City]] [[Category:Muromachi period architecture]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:Hirano-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]] }} [[sn:Kumata Shrine]] [[ja:杭全神社]]

It was originally a {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}} Shrine dedicated to the {{ill|Kumano Sansho Gongen|ja|熊野三所権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sansho Gongen|qq=}}. Its former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}} was Prefectural Shrine. It is worshipped as the clan shrine (ujigami) of the {{ill|Sakanoue clan|ja|坂上氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue clan|qq=}} and of {{ill|Hirano, Osaka|ja|平野郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirano, Osaka|qq=}}. The summer festival (Hirano Danjiri Festival) is held annually from July 11 to 14, restricting traffic on nearby {{ill|National Route 25|ja|国道25号|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Route 25|qq=}}. == History == {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan|qq=}} 4 ({{ill|862|ja|862nd year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/862|qq=}}), {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|qq=}}'s grandson {{ill|Sakanoue no Tōdō|ja|坂上当道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue no Tōdō|qq=}} established the shrine to {{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gozu Tennō|13=User:Immanuelle/Gozu Tennō|qq=}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) which became the First Main Hall. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} Main deities worshipped: * First Main Hall - {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ({{ill|Gozu Tennō|ja|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gozu Tennō|13=User:Immanuelle/Gozu Tennō|qq=}}) * Second Main Hall - It was originally a {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}} Shrine dedicated to the {{ill|Kumano Sansho Gongen|ja|熊野三所権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sansho Gongen|qq=}}. It was originally a {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}} Shrine dedicated to the {{ill|Kumano Sansho Gongen|ja|熊野三所権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sansho Gongen|qq=}}. Single-bay Kasuga-zukuri (Third Main Hall) | festival = October 17 | map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|lat_deg=34|lat_min=37|lat_sec=44.2|lon_deg=135|lon_min=33|lon_sec=17.8|label=Kumata Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} }} [[File:051223 Kumata-jinja Osaka Japan04s5.jpg|thumb|Worship Hall]] [[File:051223 Kumata-jinja Osaka Japan03s5.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Kumata Shrine''' (杭全神社, ''Kumata Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Hirano-miyamachi, Hirano-ku, Osaka. It was originally a {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}} Shrine dedicated to the {{ill|Kumano Sansho Gongen|ja|熊野三所権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sansho Gongen|qq=}}. Its former [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] was Prefectural Shrine. It is worshipped as the clan shrine (ujigami) of the {{ill|Sakanoue clan|ja|坂上氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue clan|qq=}} and of {{ill|Hirano, Osaka|ja|平野郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirano, Osaka|qq=}}. The summer festival (Hirano Danjiri Festival) is held annually from July 11 to 14, restricting traffic on nearby [[:en:Japan National Route 25|National Route 25]]. == History == [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 4 ([[:en:862|862]]), [[:en:Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]]'s grandson {{ill|Sakanoue no Tōdō|ja|坂上当道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue no Tōdō|qq=}} established the shrine to [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]] (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) which became the First Main Hall. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} Main deities worshipped: * First Main Hall - {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ([[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]]) * Second Main Hall - {{ill|Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉册尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja|速玉男尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Kotosaka-no-Mikoto|ja|事解男尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotosaka-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ({{ill|Kumano Sansho Gongen|ja|熊野三所権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sansho Gongen|qq=}}) * Third Main Hall - {{ill|Izanagi-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉諾尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ({{ill|Kumano Shōjō Gongen|ja|熊野證誠権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shōjō Gongen|qq=}}) == External Links == * [https://kumata.jp/ Kumata Shrine] [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|杭全神社|version=104231843|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka City]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Osaka City]] [[Category:Muromachi period architecture]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:Hirano-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Osaka]] }} [[sn:Kumata Shrine]] [[ja:杭全神社]] [[Category:坂上田村麻呂]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:大阪市の重要文化財]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Buildings in Hirano-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:平野区の歴史]] [[Category:Kasuga-Utsushi]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]]

{{ill|Izanagi-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉諾尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} ({{ill|Kumano Shōjō Gongen|ja|熊野證誠権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shōjō Gongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) == External Links == * [https://kumata.jp/ Kumata Shrine] [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|杭全神社|version=104231843|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:Hirano-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Osaka]] }} [[sn:Kumata Shrine]] [[ja:杭全神社]] [[Category:Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Osaka City]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Buildings in Hirano-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Hirano Ward]] [[Category:Kasuga-Utsushi]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{Commons category|Kumata jinja}}

{{ill|Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉册尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ}}

{{ill|Izanagi-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉諾尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} | type = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|lt=Fu-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} | established = [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 4 ([[:en:862|862]]) | main_hall = Single-bay [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]] (First Main Hall)
Three-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] (Second Main Hall)
Single-bay Kasuga-zukuri (Third Main Hall) | festival = October 17 | map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|lat_deg=34|lat_min=37|lat_sec=44.2|lon_deg=135|lon_min=33|lon_sec=17.8|label=Kumata Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} }} [[File:051223 Kumata-jinja Osaka Japan04s5.jpg|thumb|Worship Hall]] [[File:051223 Kumata-jinja Osaka Japan03s5.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Kumata Shrine''' (杭全神社, ''Kumata Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Hirano-miyamachi, Hirano-ku, Osaka. It was originally a {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}} Shrine dedicated to the {{ill|Kumano Sansho Gongen|ja|熊野三所権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sansho Gongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}. Its former [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] was Prefectural Shrine. It is worshipped as the clan shrine (ujigami) of the {{ill|Sakanoue clan|ja|坂上氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue clan|qq=}} and of {{ill|Hirano, Osaka|ja|平野郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirano, Osaka|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平野 (大阪市)}}. The summer festival (Hirano Danjiri Festival) is held annually from July 11 to 14, restricting traffic on nearby [[:en:Japan National Route 25|National Route 25]]. == History == [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 4 ([[:en:862|862]]), [[:en:Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]]'s grandson {{ill|Sakanoue no Tōdō|ja|坂上当道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue no Tōdō|qq=}} established the shrine to [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]] (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) which became the First Main Hall. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} Main deities worshipped: * First Main Hall - {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} ([[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]]) * Second Main Hall - {{ill|Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉册尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ}}, {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja|速玉男尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}, {{ill|Kotosaka-no-Mikoto|ja|事解男尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotosaka-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}} ({{ill|Kumano Sansho Gongen|ja|熊野三所権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sansho Gongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}) * Third Main Hall - {{ill|Izanagi-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉諾尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} ({{ill|Kumano Shōjō Gongen|ja|熊野證誠権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shōjō Gongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) == External Links == * [https://kumata.jp/ Kumata Shrine] [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|杭全神社|version=104231843|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:Hirano-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Osaka]] }} [[sn:Kumata Shrine]] [[ja:杭全神社]] [[Category:Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Osaka City]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Buildings in Hirano-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Hirano Ward]] [[Category:Kasuga-Utsushi]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{Commons category|Kumata jinja}}

{{ill|Izanagi-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉諾尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} ({{ill|Kumano Shōjō Gongen|ja|熊野證誠権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shōjō Gongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) == External Links == * [https://kumata.jp/ Kumata Shrine] [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|杭全神社|version=104231843|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:Hirano-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Osaka]] }} [[sn:Kumata Shrine]] [[ja:杭全神社]] [[Category:Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Osaka City]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Buildings in Hirano-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Hirano Ward]] [[Category:Kasuga-Utsushi]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{Commons category|Kumata jinja}}

Single-bay Kasuga-zukuri (Third Main Hall) | festival = October 17 | map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|lat_deg=34|lat_min=37|lat_sec=44.2|lon_deg=135|lon_min=33|lon_sec=17.8|label=Kumata Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} | location = {{ill|Osaka|qid=Q35765}} }} [[File:051223 Kumata-jinja Osaka Japan04s5.jpg|thumb|Worship Hall]] [[File:051223 Kumata-jinja Osaka Japan03s5.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Kumata Shrine''' (杭全神社, ''Kumata Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Hirano-miyamachi, Hirano-ku, Osaka. It was originally a {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}} Shrine dedicated to the {{ill|Kumano Sansho Gongen|ja|熊野三所権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sansho Gongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kumano Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}}. Its former [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] was Prefectural Shrine. It is worshipped as the clan shrine (ujigami) of the {{ill|Sakanoue clan|ja|坂上氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue clan|qq=|1=Sakanoue clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10928477}} and of {{ill|Hirano, Osaka|ja|平野郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirano, Osaka|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平野 (大阪市)|1=Hirano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483598}}. The summer festival (Hirano Danjiri Festival) is held annually from July 11 to 14, restricting traffic on nearby [[:en:Japan National Route 25|National Route 25]]. == History == [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 4 ([[:en:862|862]]), [[:en:Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]]'s grandson {{ill|Sakanoue no Tōdō|ja|坂上当道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue no Tōdō|qq=|1=Sakanoue no Tōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424789}} established the shrine to [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]] (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) which became the First Main Hall. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} Main deities worshipped: * First Main Hall - {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} ([[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]]) * Second Main Hall - {{ill|Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉册尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja|速玉男尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}, {{ill|Kotosaka-no-Mikoto|ja|事解男尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotosaka-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kumano Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}} ({{ill|Kumano Sansho Gongen|ja|熊野三所権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sansho Gongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kumano Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}}) * Third Main Hall - It is worshipped as the clan shrine (ujigami) of the {{ill|Sakanoue clan|ja|坂上氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue clan|qq=|1=Sakanoue clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10928477}} and of {{ill|Hirano, Osaka|ja|平野郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirano, Osaka|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平野 (大阪市)|1=Hirano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483598}}. The summer festival (Hirano Danjiri Festival) is held annually from July 11 to 14, restricting traffic on nearby [[:en:Japan National Route 25|National Route 25]]. == History == [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 4 ([[:en:862|862]]), [[:en:Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]]'s grandson {{ill|Sakanoue no Tōdō|ja|坂上当道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue no Tōdō|qq=|1=Sakanoue no Tōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424789}} established the shrine to [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]] (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) which became the First Main Hall. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} Main deities worshipped: * First Main Hall - Single-bay Kasuga-zukuri (Third Main Hall) | festival = October 17 | map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|lat_deg=34|lat_min=37|lat_sec=44.2|lon_deg=135|lon_min=33|lon_sec=17.8|label=Kumata Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} | location = {{ill|Osaka|qid=Q35765}} }} [[File:051223 Kumata-jinja Osaka Japan04s5.jpg|thumb|Worship Hall]] [[File:051223 Kumata-jinja Osaka Japan03s5.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Kumata Shrine''' (杭全神社, ''Kumata Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Hirano-miyamachi, Hirano-ku, Osaka. It was originally a {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}} Shrine dedicated to the {{ill|Kumano Sansho Gongen|ja|熊野三所権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sansho Gongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kumano Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}}. Its former [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] was Prefectural Shrine. It is worshipped as the clan shrine (ujigami) of the {{ill|Sakanoue clan|ja|坂上氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue clan|qq=|1=Sakanoue clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10928477}} and of {{ill|Hirano, Osaka|ja|平野郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirano, Osaka|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平野 (大阪市)|1=Hirano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483598}}. The summer festival (Hirano Danjiri Festival) is held annually from July 11 to 14, restricting traffic on nearby [[:en:Japan National Route 25|National Route 25]]. == History == [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 4 ([[:en:862|862]]), [[:en:Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]]'s grandson {{ill|Sakanoue no Tōdō|ja|坂上当道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue no Tōdō|qq=|1=Sakanoue no Tōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424789}} established the shrine to [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]] (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) which became the First Main Hall. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} Main deities worshipped: * First Main Hall - {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} ([[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]]) * Second Main Hall - {{ill|Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉册尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja|速玉男尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}, {{ill|Kotosaka-no-Mikoto|ja|事解男尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotosaka-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kumano Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}} ({{ill|Kumano Sansho Gongen|ja|熊野三所権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sansho Gongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kumano Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}}) * Third Main Hall - {{ill|Izanagi-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉諾尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ|1=Izanagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q813858}} ({{ill|Kumano Shōjō Gongen|ja|熊野證誠権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shōjō Gongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506335}}) == External Links == * [https://kumata.jp/ Kumata Shrine] [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|杭全神社|version=104231843|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:Hirano-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Osaka]] }} [[sn:Kumata Shrine]] [[ja:杭全神社]] [[Category:Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Osaka City]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Buildings in Hirano-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Hirano Ward]] [[Category:Kasuga-Utsushi]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[sn:Kumata Shrine]] {{Commons category|Kumata jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11523763}}

Single-bay Kasuga-zukuri (Third Main Hall) | festival = October 17 | map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|lat_deg=34|lat_min=37|lat_sec=44.2|lon_deg=135|lon_min=33|lon_sec=17.8|label=Kumata Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} | location = {{ill|Osaka|qid=Q35765}} }} [[File:051223 Kumata-jinja Osaka Japan04s5.jpg|thumb|Worship Hall]] [[File:051223 Kumata-jinja Osaka Japan03s5.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Kumata Shrine''' (杭全神社, ''Kumata Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Hirano-miyamachi, Hirano-ku, Osaka. It was originally a {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}} Shrine dedicated to the {{ill|Kumano Sansho Gongen|ja|熊野三所権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sansho Gongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kumano Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}}. Its former [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] was Prefectural Shrine. It is worshipped as the clan shrine (ujigami) of the {{ill|Sakanoue clan|ja|坂上氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue clan|qq=|1=Sakanoue clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10928477}} and of {{ill|Hirano, Osaka|ja|平野郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirano, Osaka|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平野 (大阪市)|1=Hirano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483598}}. The summer festival (Hirano Danjiri Festival) is held annually from July 11 to 14, restricting traffic on nearby [[:en:Japan National Route 25|National Route 25]]. == History == [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 4 ([[:en:862|862]]), [[:en:Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]]'s grandson {{ill|Sakanoue no Tōdō|ja|坂上当道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue no Tōdō|qq=|1=Sakanoue no Tōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424789}} established the shrine to [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]] (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) which became the First Main Hall. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} Main deities worshipped: * First Main Hall - {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} ([[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]]) * Second Main Hall - {{ill|Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉册尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja|速玉男尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}, {{ill|Kotosaka-no-Mikoto|ja|事解男尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotosaka-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kumano Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}} ({{ill|Kumano Sansho Gongen|ja|熊野三所権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sansho Gongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kumano Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}}) * Third Main Hall - {{ill|Izanagi-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉諾尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ|1=Izanagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q813858}} ({{ill|Kumano Shōjō Gongen|ja|熊野證誠権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shōjō Gongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506335}}) == External Links == * [https://kumata.jp/ Kumata Shrine] [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|杭全神社|version=104231843|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:Hirano-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Osaka]] }} [[ja:杭全神社]] [[Category:Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Osaka City]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Buildings in Hirano-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Hirano Ward]] [[Category:Kasuga-Utsushi]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{Commons category|Kumata jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11523763}}

Its former [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] was Prefectural Shrine. It is worshipped as the clan shrine (ujigami) of the {{ill|Sakanoue clan|ja|坂上氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue clan|1=Sakanoue clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10928477}} and of {{ill|Hirano, Osaka|ja|平野郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirano, Osaka|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平野 (大阪市)|1=Hirano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483598}}. The summer festival (Hirano Danjiri Festival) is held annually from July 11 to 14, restricting traffic on nearby [[:en:Japan National Route 25|National Route 25]]. == History == [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 4 ([[:en:862|862]]), [[:en:Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]]'s grandson {{ill|Sakanoue no Tōdō|ja|坂上当道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue no Tōdō|1=Sakanoue no Tōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424789}} established the shrine to [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]] (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) which became the First Main Hall. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} Main deities worshipped: * First Main Hall - {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} ([[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]]) * Second Main Hall - {{ill|Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉册尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja|速玉男尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}, {{ill|Kotosaka-no-Mikoto|ja|事解男尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotosaka-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kumano Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}} ({{ill|Kumano Sansho Gongen|ja|熊野三所権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sansho Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kumano Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}}) * Third Main Hall - {{ill|Izanagi-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉諾尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ|1=Izanagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q813858}} ({{ill|Kumano Shōjō Gongen|ja|熊野證誠権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shōjō Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506335}}) == External Links == * [https://kumata.jp/ Kumata Shrine] [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|杭全神社|version=104231843|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:Hirano-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Osaka]] }} [[ja:杭全神社]] [[Category:Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Osaka City]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Buildings in Hirano-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Hirano Ward]] [[Category:Kasuga-Utsushi]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{Commons category|Kumata jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11523763}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Single-bay Kasuga-zukuri (Third Main Hall) | festival = October 17 | map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|lat_deg=34|lat_min=37|lat_sec=44.2|lon_deg=135|lon_min=33|lon_sec=17.8|label=Kumata Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} | location = {{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}} }} [[File:051223 Kumata-jinja Osaka Japan04s5.jpg|thumb|Worship Hall]] [[File:051223 Kumata-jinja Osaka Japan03s5.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Kumata Shrine''' (杭全神社, ''Kumata Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Hirano-miyamachi, Hirano-ku, Osaka. It was originally a {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}} Shrine dedicated to the {{ill|Kumano Sansho Gongen|ja|熊野三所権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sansho Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kumano Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}}. Its former [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] was Prefectural Shrine. It is worshipped as the clan shrine (ujigami) of the {{ill|Sakanoue clan|ja|坂上氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue clan|1=Sakanoue clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10928477}} and of {{ill|Hirano, Osaka|ja|平野郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirano, Osaka|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平野 (大阪市)|1=Hirano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483598}}. The summer festival (Hirano Danjiri Festival) is held annually from July 11 to 14, restricting traffic on nearby [[:en:Japan National Route 25|National Route 25]]. == History == [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 4 ([[:en:862|862]]), [[:en:Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]]'s grandson {{ill|Sakanoue no Tōdō|ja|坂上当道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue no Tōdō|1=Sakanoue no Tōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424789}} established the shrine to [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]] (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) which became the First Main Hall. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} Main deities worshipped: * First Main Hall - {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} ([[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]]) * Second Main Hall - {{ill|Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉册尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja|速玉男尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}, {{ill|Kotosaka-no-Mikoto|ja|事解男尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotosaka-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kumano Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}} ({{ill|Kumano Sansho Gongen|ja|熊野三所権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sansho Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kumano Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}}) * Third Main Hall - {{ill|Izanagi-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉諾尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ|1=Izanagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q813858}} ({{ill|Kumano Shōjō Gongen|ja|熊野證誠権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shōjō Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506335}}) == External Links == * [https://kumata.jp/ Kumata Shrine] [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|杭全神社|version=104231843|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:Hirano-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Osaka]] }} [[ja:杭全神社]] [[Category:Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Osaka City]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Buildings in Hirano-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Hirano Ward]] [[Category:Kasuga-Utsushi]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{Commons category|Kumata jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11523763}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

{{ill|Izanagi-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉諾尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ|1=Izanagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q813858}} | type = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | established = [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 4 ([[:en:862|862]]) | main_hall = Single-bay [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]] (First Main Hall)
Three-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] (Second Main Hall)
Single-bay Kasuga-zukuri (Third Main Hall) | festival = October 17 | map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|lat_deg=34|lat_min=37|lat_sec=44.2|lon_deg=135|lon_min=33|lon_sec=17.8|label=Kumata Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} | location = {{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}} }} [[File:051223 Kumata-jinja Osaka Japan04s5.jpg|thumb|Worship Hall]] [[File:051223 Kumata-jinja Osaka Japan03s5.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Kumata Shrine''' (杭全神社, ''Kumata Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Hirano-miyamachi, Hirano-ku, Osaka. It was originally a {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}} Shrine dedicated to the {{ill|Kumano Sansho Gongen|ja|熊野三所権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sansho Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kumano Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}}. Its former [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] was Prefectural Shrine. It is worshipped as the clan shrine (ujigami) of the {{ill|Sakanoue clan|ja|坂上氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue clan|1=Sakanoue clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10928477}} and of {{ill|Hirano, Osaka|ja|平野郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirano, Osaka|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平野 (大阪市)|1=Hirano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483598}}. The summer festival (Hirano Danjiri Festival) is held annually from July 11 to 14, restricting traffic on nearby [[:en:Japan National Route 25|National Route 25]]. == History == [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 4 ([[:en:862|862]]), [[:en:Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]]'s grandson {{ill|Sakanoue no Tōdō|ja|坂上当道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue no Tōdō|1=Sakanoue no Tōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424789}} established the shrine to [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]] (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) which became the First Main Hall. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} Main deities worshipped: * First Main Hall - {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} ([[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]]) * Second Main Hall - {{ill|Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉册尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja|速玉男尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}, {{ill|Kotosaka-no-Mikoto|ja|事解男尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotosaka-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kumano Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}} ({{ill|Kumano Sansho Gongen|ja|熊野三所権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sansho Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kumano Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}}) * Third Main Hall - {{ill|Izanagi-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉諾尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ|1=Izanagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q813858}} ({{ill|Kumano Shōjō Gongen|ja|熊野證誠権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shōjō Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506335}}) == External Links == * [https://kumata.jp/ Kumata Shrine] [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|杭全神社|version=104231843|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:Hirano-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Osaka]] }} [[ja:杭全神社]] [[Category:Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Osaka City]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Buildings in Hirano-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Hirano Ward]] [[Category:Kasuga-Utsushi]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{Commons category|Kumata jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11523763}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

{{ill|Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉册尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}
{{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja|速玉男尊|1=Kumano Gongen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|13=User:Immanuelle/熊野権現|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}}
{{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kotosaka-no-Mikoto|ja|事解男尊|1=Kumano Gongen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|13=User:Immanuelle/熊野権現|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}}
{{ill|Izanagi-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉諾尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ|1=Izanagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q813858}} | type = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | established = [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 4 ([[:en:862|862]]) | main_hall = Single-bay [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]] (First Main Hall)
Three-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] (Second Main Hall)
Single-bay Kasuga-zukuri (Third Main Hall) | festival = October 17 | map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|lat_deg=34|lat_min=37|lat_sec=44.2|lon_deg=135|lon_min=33|lon_sec=17.8|label=Kumata Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} | location = {{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}} }} [[File:051223 Kumata-jinja Osaka Japan04s5.jpg|thumb|Worship Hall]] [[File:051223 Kumata-jinja Osaka Japan03s5.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Kumata Shrine''' (杭全神社, ''Kumata Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Hirano-miyamachi, Hirano-ku, Osaka. It was originally a {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}} Shrine dedicated to the {{ill|Kumano Sansho Gongen|ja|熊野三所権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sansho Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kumano Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}}. Its former [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]] was Prefectural Shrine. It is worshipped as the clan shrine (ujigami) of the {{ill|Sakanoue clan|ja|坂上氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue clan|1=Sakanoue clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10928477}} and of {{ill|Hirano, Osaka|ja|平野郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirano, Osaka|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平野 (大阪市)|1=Hirano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483598}}. The summer festival (Hirano Danjiri Festival) is held annually from July 11 to 14, restricting traffic on nearby [[:en:Japan National Route 25|National Route 25]]. == History == [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 4 ([[:en:862|862]]), [[:en:Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]]'s grandson {{ill|Sakanoue no Tōdō|ja|坂上当道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue no Tōdō|1=Sakanoue no Tōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424789}} established the shrine to [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]] (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) which became the First Main Hall. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} Main deities worshipped: * First Main Hall - {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} ([[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]]) * Second Main Hall - {{ill|Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉册尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja|速玉男尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}, {{ill|Kotosaka-no-Mikoto|ja|事解男尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotosaka-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kumano Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}} ({{ill|Kumano Sansho Gongen|ja|熊野三所権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sansho Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kumano Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}}) * Third Main Hall - {{ill|Izanagi-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉諾尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ|1=Izanagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q813858}} ({{ill|Kumano Shōjō Gongen|ja|熊野證誠権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shōjō Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506335}}) == External Links == * [https://kumata.jp/ Kumata Shrine] {{translated page|ja|杭全神社|version=104231843|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:杭全神社]] {{Commons category|Kumata jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11523763}} [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Buildings in Hirano-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Osaka]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Hirano-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Hirano Ward]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Osaka City]] [[Category:Kasuga-Utsushi]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

It is worshipped as the clan shrine (ujigami) of the {{ill|Sakanoue clan|ja|坂上氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue clan|1=Sakanoue clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10928477}} and of {{ill|Hirano, Osaka|ja|平野郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirano, Osaka|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平野 (大阪市)|1=Hirano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483598}}. The summer festival (Hirano Danjiri Festival) is held annually from July 11 to 14, restricting traffic on nearby [[:en:Japan National Route 25|National Route 25]]. == History == [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 4 ([[:en:862|862]]), [[:en:Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]]'s grandson {{ill|Sakanoue no Tōdō|ja|坂上当道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue no Tōdō|1=Sakanoue no Tōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424789}} established the shrine to [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]] (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) which became the First Main Hall. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} Main deities worshipped: * First Main Hall - {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} ([[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]]) * Second Main Hall - {{ill|Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉册尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja|速玉男尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}, {{ill|Kotosaka-no-Mikoto|ja|事解男尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotosaka-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kumano Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}} ({{ill|Kumano Sansho Gongen|ja|熊野三所権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sansho Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kumano Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}}) * Third Main Hall - {{ill|Izanagi-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉諾尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ|1=Izanagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q813858}} ({{ill|Kumano Shōjō Gongen|ja|熊野證誠権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shōjō Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506335}}) == External Links == * [https://kumata.jp/ Kumata Shrine] {{translated page|ja|杭全神社|version=104231843|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:杭全神社]] {{Commons category|Kumata jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11523763}} [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Buildings in Hirano-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Osaka]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Hirano-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Hirano Ward]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Osaka City]] [[Category:Kasuga-Utsushi]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

{{ill|Izanagi-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉諾尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ|1=Izanagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q813858}} | type = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|1=Metropolitan Shrine|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | established = {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan}} 4 ({{ill|862|en|862|WD=Q24993|lt=862}}) | main_hall = Single-bay {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|en|Kasuga-zukuri|WD=Q3193781|lt=Kasuga-zukuri}} (First Main Hall)
Three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri}} (Second Main Hall)
Single-bay Kasuga-zukuri (Third Main Hall) | festival = October 17 | map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|lat_deg=34|lat_min=37|lat_sec=44.2|lon_deg=135|lon_min=33|lon_sec=17.8|label=Kumata Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} | location = {{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}} }} [[File:051223 Kumata-jinja Osaka Japan04s5.jpg|thumb|Worship Hall]] [[File:051223 Kumata-jinja Osaka Japan03s5.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Kumata Shrine''' (杭全神社, ''Kumata Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in Hirano-miyamachi, Hirano-ku, Osaka. It was originally a {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}} Shrine dedicated to the {{ill|Kumano Sansho Gongen|ja|熊野三所権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sansho Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kumano Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}}. Its former {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was Prefectural Shrine. It is worshipped as the clan shrine (ujigami) of the {{ill|Sakanoue clan|ja|坂上氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue clan|1=Sakanoue clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10928477}} and of {{ill|Hirano, Osaka|ja|平野郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirano, Osaka|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平野 (大阪市)|1=Hirano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483598}}. The summer festival (Hirano Danjiri Festival) is held annually from July 11 to 14, restricting traffic on nearby {{ill|Japan National Route 25|en|Japan National Route 25|WD=Q900338|lt=National Route 25}}. == History == {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan}} 4 ({{ill|862|en|862|WD=Q24993|lt=862}}), {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|en|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|WD=Q941979|lt=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro}}'s grandson {{ill|Sakanoue no Tōdō|ja|坂上当道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue no Tōdō|1=Sakanoue no Tōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424789}} established the shrine to {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) which became the First Main Hall. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} Main deities worshipped: * First Main Hall - {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} ({{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō}}) * Second Main Hall - {{ill|Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉册尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja|速玉男尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}, {{ill|Kotosaka-no-Mikoto|ja|事解男尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotosaka-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kumano Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}} ({{ill|Kumano Sansho Gongen|ja|熊野三所権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sansho Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kumano Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}}) * Third Main Hall - {{ill|Izanagi-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉諾尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ|1=Izanagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q813858}} ({{ill|Kumano Shōjō Gongen|ja|熊野證誠権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shōjō Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506335}}) == External Links == * [https://kumata.jp/ Kumata Shrine] {{translated page|ja|杭全神社|version=104231843|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:杭全神社]] {{Commons category|Kumata jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11523763}} [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Buildings in Hirano-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Osaka]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Hirano-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Hirano Ward]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Osaka City]] [[Category:Kasuga-Utsushi]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Kumata jinja]][[ja:杭全神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Kumata jinja]][[ja:杭全神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kumata jinja]][[ja:杭全神社]] {{Infobox religious building| | Name = Kumata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:051223 Kumata-jinja Osaka Japan02bs45.jpg | location = {{ill|Hirano-miyamachi|ja|平野宮町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirano-miyamachi|1=Hirano-miyamachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q88985031}}, {{ill|Hirano-ku, Osaka|en|Hirano-ku, Osaka|WD=Q861256|lt=Hirano-ku, Osaka|1=Hirano-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka|1=Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, 2-chome 1-67 | coordinates = {{Coord|34.62894444|N|135.55494444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
{{ill|Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉册尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}
{{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja|速玉男尊|1=Kumano Gongen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|13=User:Immanuelle/熊野権現|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}}
{{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kotosaka-no-Mikoto|ja|事解男尊|1=Kumano Gongen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|13=User:Immanuelle/熊野権現|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}}
{{ill|Izanagi-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉諾尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ|1=Izanagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q813858}} | type = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|1=Fu-sha|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | established = {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan|1=Zhenguan}} 4 ({{ill|862|en|862|WD=Q24993|lt=862|1=862}}) | main_hall = Single-bay {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|en|Kasuga-zukuri|WD=Q3193781|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} (First Main Hall)
Three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} (Second Main Hall)
Single-bay Kasuga-zukuri (Third Main Hall) | festival = October 17 | map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|lat_deg=34|lat_min=37|lat_sec=44.2|lon_deg=135|lon_min=33|lon_sec=17.8|label=Kumata Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} | location = {{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765|1=Osaka}} }} [[File:051223 Kumata-jinja Osaka Japan04s5.jpg|thumb|Worship Hall]] [[File:051223 Kumata-jinja Osaka Japan03s5.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Kumata Shrine''' (杭全神社, ''Kumata Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Hirano-miyamachi, Hirano-ku, Osaka. It was originally a {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}} Shrine dedicated to the {{ill|Kumano Sansho Gongen|ja|熊野三所権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sansho Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kumano Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}}. Its former {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was Prefectural Shrine. It is worshipped as the clan shrine (ujigami) of the {{ill|Sakanoue clan|ja|坂上氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue clan|1=Sakanoue clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10928477}} and of {{ill|Hirano, Osaka|ja|平野郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirano, Osaka|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平野 (大阪市)|1=Hirano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483598}}. The summer festival (Hirano Danjiri Festival) is held annually from July 11 to 14, restricting traffic on nearby {{ill|Japan National Route 25|en|Japan National Route 25|WD=Q900338|lt=National Route 25|1=Japan National Route 25}}. == History == {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan|1=Zhenguan}} 4 ({{ill|862|en|862|WD=Q24993|lt=862|1=862}}), {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|en|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|WD=Q941979|lt=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|1=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro}}'s grandson {{ill|Sakanoue no Tōdō|ja|坂上当道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue no Tōdō|1=Sakanoue no Tōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424789}} established the shrine to {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) which became the First Main Hall. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} Main deities worshipped: * First Main Hall - {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} ({{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō}}) * Second Main Hall - {{ill|Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉册尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja|速玉男尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}, {{ill|Kotosaka-no-Mikoto|ja|事解男尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotosaka-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kumano Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}} ({{ill|Kumano Sansho Gongen|ja|熊野三所権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sansho Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kumano Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}}) * Third Main Hall - {{ill|Izanagi-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉諾尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ|1=Izanagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q813858}} ({{ill|Kumano Shōjō Gongen|ja|熊野證誠権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shōjō Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506335|1=Q135506335}}) == External Links == * [https://kumata.jp/ Kumata Shrine] {{translated page|ja|杭全神社|version=104231843|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:杭全神社]] {{Commons category|Kumata jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11523763}} [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Buildings in Hirano-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Osaka]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Hirano-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Hirano Ward]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Osaka City]] [[Category:Kasuga-Utsushi]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

Single-bay Kasuga-zukuri (Third Main Hall) | festival = October 17 | map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|lat_deg=34|lat_min=37|lat_sec=44.2|lon_deg=135|lon_min=33|lon_sec=17.8|label=Kumata Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} | location = {{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765|1=Osaka}} }} [[File:051223 Kumata-jinja Osaka Japan04s5.jpg|thumb|Worship Hall]] [[File:051223 Kumata-jinja Osaka Japan03s5.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Kumata Shrine''' (杭全神社, ''Kumata Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Hirano-miyamachi, Hirano-ku, Osaka. It was originally a {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}} Shrine dedicated to the {{ill|Kumano Sansho Gongen|ja|熊野三所権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sansho Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kumano Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}}. Its former {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was Prefectural Shrine. It is worshipped as the clan shrine (ujigami) of the {{ill|Sakanoue clan|ja|坂上氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue clan|1=Sakanoue clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10928477}} and of {{ill|Hirano, Osaka|ja|平野郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirano, Osaka|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平野 (大阪市)|1=Hirano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483598}}. The summer festival (Hirano Danjiri Festival) is held annually from July 11 to 14, restricting traffic on nearby {{ill|Japan National Route 25|en|Japan National Route 25|WD=Q900338|lt=National Route 25|1=Japan National Route 25}}. == History == {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan|1=Zhenguan}} 4 ({{ill|862|en|862|WD=Q24993|lt=862|1=862}}), {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|en|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|WD=Q941979|lt=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|1=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro}}'s grandson {{ill|Sakanoue no Tōdō|ja|坂上当道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue no Tōdō|1=Sakanoue no Tōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424789}} established the shrine to {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) which became the First Main Hall. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} Main deities worshipped: * First Main Hall - [[co:commons:Category:Kumata jinja]][[ja:杭全神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Kumata jinja]][[ja:杭全神社]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Kumata jinja]][[ja:杭全神社]] {{Infobox religious building| | Name = Kumata Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:051223 Kumata-jinja Osaka Japan02bs45.jpg | location = {{ill|Hirano-miyamachi|ja|平野宮町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirano-miyamachi|1=Hirano-miyamachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q88985031}}, {{ill|Hirano-ku, Osaka|en|Hirano-ku, Osaka|WD=Q861256|lt=Hirano-ku, Osaka|1=Hirano-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|WD=Q35765|lt=Osaka|1=Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, 2-chome 1-67 | coordinates = {{Coord|34.62894444|N|135.55494444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
{{ill|Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉册尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}
{{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja|速玉男尊|1=Kumano Gongen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|13=User:Immanuelle/熊野権現|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}}
{{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kotosaka-no-Mikoto|ja|事解男尊|1=Kumano Gongen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|13=User:Immanuelle/熊野権現|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}}
{{ill|Izanagi-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉諾尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ|1=Izanagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q813858}} | type = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|1=Fu-sha|WD=Q134917326}}{{efn|lt=Fu-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | established = {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan|1=Zhenguan}} 4 ({{ill|862|en|862|WD=Q24993|lt=862|1=862}}) | main_hall = Single-bay {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|en|Kasuga-zukuri|WD=Q3193781|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} (First Main Hall)
Three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} (Second Main Hall)
Single-bay Kasuga-zukuri (Third Main Hall) | festival = October 17 | map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|lat_deg=34|lat_min=37|lat_sec=44.2|lon_deg=135|lon_min=33|lon_sec=17.8|label=Kumata Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} | location = {{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765|1=Osaka}} }} [[File:051223 Kumata-jinja Osaka Japan04s5.jpg|thumb|Worship Hall]] [[File:051223 Kumata-jinja Osaka Japan03s5.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Kumata Shrine''' (杭全神社, ''Kumata Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Hirano-miyamachi, Hirano-ku, Osaka. It was originally a {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}} Shrine dedicated to the {{ill|Kumano Sansho Gongen|ja|熊野三所権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sansho Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kumano Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}}. Its former {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was Prefectural Shrine. It is worshipped as the clan shrine (ujigami) of the {{ill|Sakanoue clan|ja|坂上氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue clan|1=Sakanoue clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10928477}} and of {{ill|Hirano, Osaka|ja|平野郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirano, Osaka|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平野 (大阪市)|1=Hirano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483598}}. The summer festival (Hirano Danjiri Festival) is held annually from July 11 to 14, restricting traffic on nearby {{ill|Japan National Route 25|en|Japan National Route 25|WD=Q900338|lt=National Route 25|1=Japan National Route 25}}. == History == {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan|1=Zhenguan}} 4 ({{ill|862|en|862|WD=Q24993|lt=862|1=862}}), {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|en|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|WD=Q941979|lt=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|1=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro}}'s grandson {{ill|Sakanoue no Tōdō|ja|坂上当道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue no Tōdō|1=Sakanoue no Tōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424789}} established the shrine to {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) which became the First Main Hall. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} Main deities worshipped: * First Main Hall - {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} ({{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō}}) * Second Main Hall - {{ill|Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉册尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja|速玉男尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}, {{ill|Kotosaka-no-Mikoto|ja|事解男尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotosaka-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kumano Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}} ({{ill|Kumano Sansho Gongen|ja|熊野三所権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sansho Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kumano Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}}) * Third Main Hall - {{ill|Izanagi-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉諾尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ|1=Izanagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q813858}} ({{ill|Kumano Shōjō Gongen|ja|熊野證誠権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shōjō Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506335|1=Q135506335}}) == External Links == * [https://kumata.jp/ Kumata Shrine] {{translated page|ja|杭全神社|version=104231843|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:杭全神社]] {{Commons category|Kumata jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11523763}} [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hirano-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Osaka]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Hirano-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Hirano Ward]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Osaka City]] [[Category:Kasuga-Utsushi]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Single-bay Kasuga-zukuri (Third Main Hall) | festival = October 17 | map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|lat_deg=34|lat_min=37|lat_sec=44.2|lon_deg=135|lon_min=33|lon_sec=17.8|label=Kumata Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} | location = {{ill|Osaka|qid=Q35765|1=Osaka}} }} [[File:051223 Kumata-jinja Osaka Japan04s5.jpg|thumb|Worship Hall]] [[File:051223 Kumata-jinja Osaka Japan03s5.jpg|thumb|Main Hall]] '''Kumata Shrine''' (杭全神社, ''Kumata Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Hirano-miyamachi, Hirano-ku, Osaka. It was originally a {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}} Shrine dedicated to the {{ill|Kumano Sansho Gongen|ja|熊野三所権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sansho Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kumano Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11568925}}. Its former {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qid=Q712534|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was Prefectural Shrine. It is worshipped as the clan shrine (ujigami) of the {{ill|Sakanoue clan|ja|坂上氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue clan|1=Sakanoue clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10928477}} and of {{ill|Hirano, Osaka|ja|平野郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirano, Osaka|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平野 (大阪市)|1=Hirano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11483598}}. The summer festival (Hirano Danjiri Festival) is held annually from July 11 to 14, restricting traffic on nearby {{ill|Japan National Route 25|en|Japan National Route 25|qid=Q900338|lt=National Route 25|1=Japan National Route 25}}. == History == {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|qid=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan|1=Zhenguan}} 4 ({{ill|862|en|862|qid=Q24993|lt=862|1=862}}), {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|en|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|qid=Q941979|lt=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|1=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro}}'s grandson {{ill|Sakanoue no Tōdō|ja|坂上当道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue no Tōdō|1=Sakanoue no Tōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11424789}} established the shrine to {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|qid=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) which became the First Main Hall. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} Main deities worshipped: * First Main Hall - {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} ({{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|qid=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō}}) * Second Main Hall - {{ill|Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉册尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q682306}}, {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja|速玉男尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}, {{ill|Kotosaka-no-Mikoto|ja|事解男尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotosaka-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kumano Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11568925}} ({{ill|Kumano Sansho Gongen|ja|熊野三所権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Sansho Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kumano Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11568925}}) * Third Main Hall - {{ill|Izanagi-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉諾尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ|1=Izanagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q813858}} ({{ill|Kumano Shōjō Gongen|ja|熊野證誠権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Shōjō Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506335|1=Q135506335}}) == External Links == * [https://kumata.jp/ Kumata Shrine] {{translated page|ja|杭全神社|version=104231843|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:杭全神社]] {{Commons category|Kumata jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11523763}} [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hirano-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Osaka]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Hirano-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Hirano Ward]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Osaka City]] [[Category:Kasuga-Utsushi]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
